explanations.1: Horizontal and vertical motions are independent, so gravity acts on both balls equally in the vertical direction regardless of horizontal velocity.
explanations.2: Using the Pythagorean theorem for perpendicular vectors, the resultant displacement is the square root of (3.0² + 4.0²), which equals 5.0 blocks.
explanations.3: By definition, projectile motion is the motion of an object subject only to the acceleration of gravity.
explanations.4: The head-to-tail method is the standard graphical way to add vectors by connecting the tip of the first to the tail of the second.
explanations.5: The negative of a vector has the same magnitude as the original but points in the exactly opposite direction.
explanations.6: Multiplying a vector by a negative scalar changes its magnitude by the absolute value of the scalar and reverses its direction.
explanations.7: Vector addition is commutative, meaning the same resultant is achieved regardless of the sequence in which vectors are combined.
explanations.8: The horizontal component is calculated using Ax = A cos θ, and 10.0 m * cos(30°) is approximately 8.66 m.
explanations.9: The magnitude is found using the Pythagorean theorem: the square root of (6.0² + 8.0²) equals 10.0 m.
explanations.10: Analytical methods provide higher accuracy and precision because they rely on mathematical functions rather than the physical measurements of a hand-drawn diagram.
explanations.11: The angle is found using θ = tan⁻¹(Ay/Ax), and tan⁻¹(5/5) is tan⁻¹(1), which is 45 degrees.
explanations.12: Because gravity only acts vertically, there is no horizontal acceleration, meaning the horizontal velocity remains unchanged throughout the motion.
explanations.13: At the highest point of the flight, the projectile momentarily stops rising before it begins to fall, so its vertical velocity is zero.
explanations.14: Neglecting air resistance, the maximum range for any projectile on level ground is always achieved at an angle of 45 degrees.
explanations.15: For projectiles on level ground, angles that add up to 90 degrees (complementary angles) produce the same horizontal range.
explanations.16: Standard projectile motion analysis uses perpendicular axes where the horizontal axis follows the motion and the vertical axis follows the direction of gravity.
explanations.17: Galileo correctly analyzed projectile motion as the combination of uniform horizontal motion and naturally accelerated vertical motion.
explanations.18: The total velocity is the vector sum of the boat's velocity and the river's velocity, which results in a diagonal path toward the north-east.
explanations.19: The ground speed is the magnitude of the resultant velocity vector: the square root of (40² + 30²), which is 50 m/s.
explanations.20: Classical relativity provides an excellent approximation for motion at speeds significantly lower than the speed of light, specifically under 1%.
explanations.21: In one-dimensional relative motion, velocities in the same direction add like ordinary numbers: 5.0 + 1.0 = 6.0 m/s.
explanations.22: Displacement is the straight-line distance between the starting and ending points, whereas 14 blocks is the total path length traveled.
explanations.23: The fundamental principle of two-dimensional kinematics is that motion along perpendicular axes does not affect motion along the other axis.
explanations.24: Maximum height is strictly a function of the initial vertical velocity and the acceleration due to gravity, independent of horizontal motion.
